Tolan's Darkwood Gauntlets  
 

Lore Item
Slot: HANDS
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 14
Effect: Snare (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 4.0) at Level 45
WT: 5.0 Size: SMALL
Class: RNG
Race: ALL except IKS VAH FRG
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Charges: Unlimited

#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 09 2002 at 12:42 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ok I am planning on making a Rouge and I decided that my race is going to be a dwarf, I basically aiming for huge damage backstabs, so the extra strength would help, also it seems like most of everyone who is a Rouge is a Halfling so i figured i would be different. I was originally planning on making the character on Sullon or Vallon Zek, yet i have heard from quite a few people that Rouges suck at PVP. Any thoughts?
RE: Race and PVP for Rouges
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2002 at 5:00 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770260749
Scholar
104 posts
Sorry, but why did you post all that here? You can't wear this as a rouge or any dwarf for that matter, and there is no STR on these. Sorry if I am out of line or missing something here.

Safe Travels
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 08 2002 at 2:10 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Just wanted te point out an error. Dwarfs, Ogres, and Trolls cannot be rangers. Therefor they shouldn't be able to equip this item ;)
RE: ERROR
QuoteReply
# May 23 2002 at 3:47 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591772489776
10 posts
It's only listed as all the extra races because of the amount of coding it would take to remove them. I for one am glad they would leave something like alone and work on the stuff that matters.
▲ Page top
Hotbox
QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2001 at 12:46 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250863344
27 posts
How do you make a hot box for a right-clickable item?
▲ Page top
RE: Hotbox
QuoteReply
# Jan 15 2002 at 10:48 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591763776605
Scholar
29 posts
1. Open your inventory.
2. Left Click and HOLD on the item you want to make a button for.
3. A button will appear on your cursor for that inventory slot. Place this on your hotkey bar.
▲ Page top

36 posts

no

# Dec 16 2001 at 6:13 PM Rating: Decent

you must remain stationary

it takes 2x as long or longer to cast as normal snare

proc's at level 50 i believe

# Feb 08 2002 at 11:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361516680

19 posts
Been using this item on my ranger for several levels now, you can only use the effect once you reach 45 and the cast time is slightly slower than the normal snare spell, I would say more around 10% longer.

i agree these r slow and prob the mostuselespiece of tolans snare why not just cast it? is quicker and take nearly no mana at this lv it procs????

▲ Page top
Gaunts
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2001 at 11:58 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I would have to agree on the "cast time" issue. It is faulkin slow! It's been ok for me if not pulling, and I know I can afford the time it takes to cast, but as far as getting yourself saved by these, it aint gonna happen. AC is nice, but I'm kind of dissapointed at the lack of any stats. So, don't count on these things to save you as you run away, (heh, imagine that, a Ranger getting beaten?), if you can afford the time to cast, make it a hotbox and you now have another spell slot open. My 1.5cp

Ranger Of the 45th Beatdown
Ayonae Ro

QuoteReply
# Jul 29 2001 at 5:28 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757837736
6 posts
well thies things dont proc till like 50 or somin so its rity much just there for ac, and thats good and all but be the time u can use the effecdt u probably could get somin wiht that much ac or even more and lets see some gloves do tons and tons of dam. that wurmy is prity good, i love mine it great(yes i am a twink. yes i am sorry.) if u have wurmy and ur stamina is over a 100 u can do prity good wiht it, and because of its masive dam fingst dont last that long so stamina isnt a big thing, and if the fight dose last long enuff for ur stamina to drain then that means u shouldnt be fightin it or u are in a group.

61 posts
ahh you are so young these things r better then a wurmy a wurmy sucks post 43 before 43 ist not that bad (want more info visit the wurmy page i have posts there) but still tolans guants r better altho ucanget these for bat 1200-1500 on the rathe and wurmy is 1700-2200 and if you come to KC adn fight the lv 45 drovvys it will take long enough to draineven somone with 200 sta to 0 with a wurmy it would take you to prob 0 a few times at your sta. and these things proc at either 45 or 46 i am almost psositive its 46. anyways go to the wurmy page ;-)

# Aug 19 2001 at 2:52 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248322736
28 posts
I would not trade them. But contrary to popular brlief I am a ranger & have no problem with a Wurmmy & my crappy stamina of 105.....It is a spell called invigor...Learn to use your class to it's full ability! It is a lvl 30 spell that cost 20m & a 3.5 cast time. Keeps the sta from going down, or brings it back up..


Rangers Own!
▲ Page top
RE: trade gloves?
QuoteReply
# Jan 19 2002 at 9:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761107879
Scholar
22 posts
Hold on to your guantlets. As a Ranger we get ganked on AC. Every point counts keep adding. The advantage of the glove proc is that it can not be interupted. Hey if you time it right you won't even have to break stride.

WS's go down in price daily. They are down to about 1k on E'ci. I do like mine. So many people say it sux. That has helped to drive down the price.

If you get your wurmy, keep invig memmed and zing yourself as needed. If you are the puller, invig when mob turns let and grab the next.

Jiblet
Ranger
E'ci
▲ Page top
RE: trade gloves?
QuoteReply
# Jul 21 2001 at 2:18 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762308791
25 posts
I wouldnt do it. A wurmy isnt very good for a ranger because it takes alot of stamina. Pls its good ac.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jul 19 2001 at 9:09 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post)
Not useless, just depends on situation
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2001 at 8:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965813989
15 posts
I don't find these useless at all - first off, the AC on them is nice and we all know how rangers are lacking in that department - second of all, the snare effect might take a long time to cast, but for pulling when out of mana, or doing emergency/gamble snares so you can run your *** off to the zone, these are nice.

I personally only use the effect if I'm being chased by something (Take an ulthork for example) and have enough health that I can afford to wait for the gloves to cast, and take a couple of hits in doing so, gambling that the spell won't get interrupted. I've trained myself to have SoW up at all times as well, so these gloves have probably saved my life while out of mana on more than one occasion :)

That, and try finding another plate look pair of gloves for your ranger - hehe not easy ;)

- Jerhyn Darkrain -
- 48 Ranger of Tunare -
- Wind Riders -
▲ Page top
Effect isnt good but
QuoteReply
# Jun 16 2001 at 9:48 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Rangers should almost always us their own snare/ensnare spells over this, even if you are pulling people in your group will be like WTF? 10 seconds is a long time to cast for such a low mana req spell, and if the mob is on the move it will probably be out of range by the time the 10 seconds is up, more than anything get this for the AC. The effect is fun to use when your bored casting on n00b mobs tho when you are stuck in EC all day auctioning lol.
▲ Page top
level 45 and useless
QuoteReply
# Jun 11 2001 at 6:44 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Yes you can put these on a hot button, but the cast time is ten seconds. So if something is chasing you, you will never get this cast off in time. These will get you killed more than they will save you.
